description: Registry of clause tags for docs that carry stable clause identifiers and normative language. Tags: 'check-exists' — a named, runnable check already guards the clause (check-ref points at it); 'checkable' — a mechanical check can guard the clause but is not built yet (check-ref names the intended check and any interim probe); 'judgment-only' — compliance is a per-change judgment no check can decide (justification says why). Clause identifiers are stable: never renumbered, and a retired identifier stays reserved (listed under 'retired').

clauses.justification: Whether a novel event source records what the user did or what the code did is a per-change design judgment; no check can classify sources that do not exist yet.

clauses.check-ref: Intended: a static check that recorder/service-worker event registrations stay within the enumerated capture surface (not yet built). Interim probe: the scripted-truth corpus diff (scripts/corpus-compare.js via npm run corpus:check and corpus:check:desktop) reddens when an effect enters a scripted session's stream.

clauses.justification: Whether a piece of observed context describes the actions faithfully and is worth recording alongside them is a design judgment per capture feature.
Whether a new field is context or an action-stream entry in disguise is judgment; no check can decide a field's meaning from its shape.
Applying the definition to a novel input class — is it a physical input the user deliberately performed? — is judgment.
Classifying a novel consequence as effect versus action is judgment; no check can classify consequences that do not exist yet.
Choosing what stands in as the proxy for an out-of-visibility action is a per-proxy design judgment.
Which effect is the immediate one in a new proxy's causal chain is judgment; regressions on existing proxies are probed by the scripted-truth corpus.
Known duplicate pairs are suppressed and unit-tested per instance, but at-most-one-proxy for a newly designed proxy is judgment.
Whether a proxy's recorded shape identifies the user action is judgment.

clauses.check-ref: Scripted-truth corpus redaction sessions ext-redaction and d-redaction (scripts/corpus-compare.js via npm run corpus:check and corpus:check:desktop) verify mask + redacted flag end-to-end on both platforms; the shared detection util packages/shared/lib/field-sensitivity.js is unit-tested; scripts/sufficiency-lint.js enforces the locator masking the contract promises.

clauses.justification: Whether a detection signal is strong enough to trigger masking is a design judgment; no check can decide signal strength.
Applying the signals to a mixed case (the table's Depends rows) or to a signal the table does not list is judgment; no check can decide such a case.

clauses.check-ref: packages/shared/tests/unit/no-resolution-execution.test.js greps the shipped runtime and build-tooling paths for identifiers unique to the ordered procedure (the corroboration guard, the per-candidate verdict, the containment filter); run via npm run test:shared. The test trees are outside its scan scope — their non-execution is held by the vector machinery’s design (the "resolved" property emerges from counts and equalities, never computed) and by review.

clauses.justification: Whether a new use of recorded match statistics stays a conservative eligibility exclusion or starts deciding outcomes is a per-change judgment about the specification.

No check can observe a consumer implementation’s evaluation order, and an outcome-invariance test would itself execute the procedure, which LR-1 forbids to every Docent artifact; the hygiene locks’ per-candidate re-derivations are order-free by construction, probing only the repo-side surface.
Whether an identity definition for a new platform counts the same distinct elements is judgment; no check can compare automation engines that do not exist yet.

Whether a novel scope or frame construct is uniquely bound is judgment; the no-candidates routing is probed operationally by the sufficiency lint (scripts/sufficiency-lint.js, element-locators predicate).
Whether a schema or specification change keeps entries independent candidates is a semantics judgment no check can decide.
No check can observe a consumer implementation’s evaluation order; the hygiene locks probe the repo-side consequence (every eligible candidate accounted for over committed snapshots).

clauses.check-ref: The hygiene locks (packages/shared/tests/unit/conformance-vectors.test.js) evaluate exactly the eligible set — not masked, match_index not null — over every committed vector snapshot, so the eligibility definition is executable and executed; run via npm run test:shared.

clauses.justification: Whether a new procedure step keeps ineligible entries out of selection and corroboration is judgment about specification edits.

clauses.check-ref: Intended: a check that parses the strategy tables and pins them against the vector-measurement evaluators (not built). Interim probe: lock 5’s query evaluator (packages/shared/tests/unit/conformance-vectors.test.js with packages/shared/tests/unit/vector-measurement.js / packages/shared/tests/unit/vector-measurement-desktop.js; run via npm run test:shared) implements the Application step per strategy over committed snapshots — alignment between the doc tables and the evaluator remains a review judgment.

The coverage-ledger lock (corpus/vectors-coverage.json, locked in packages/shared/tests/unit/conformance-vectors.test.js) ties every emitted strategy to a committed vector and keeps reserved strategies outside vector scope; run via npm run test:shared.

clauses.justification: Which recorded facts corroborate and which contradict in a novel case is judgment; lock 6 probes the committed vectors’ tag/text agreement with element_facts.
Applying tag equality to a novel control-type mapping is judgment; lock 6 checks committed vectors’ tag agreement exactly.
Whether a text comparison stays a normalized containment test under a specification edit is judgment; lock 6 checks committed vectors’ text containment, including the vacuous-null case.
Whether a field has discriminating power in a novel corroboration case is judgment; no check can decide signal strength.
Whether a specification edit preserves the verdict mapping is judgment; the locks probe only the committed vectors’ counts.
Whether a containment-filter change still operates on recorded facts only, and never collapses beyond what they disambiguate, is judgment.
Whether a new failure mode folds into the three outcomes or mints a fourth is judgment.
Whether diagnostics stay informative rather than decision-bearing is judgment about specification edits.
Whether a proposed vector’s outcome depends on a refinement beyond the procedure is judgment at vector review.
Whether a worked example drifted from the algorithm is judgment at review; the algorithm-governs rule decides the repair direction, not whether drift occurred.

clauses.check-ref: The vector machinery makes the definition executable for the published vectors: lock 5 re-derives every candidate’s matches over the committed snapshot and the produce-stage oracle asserts produced == committed (packages/shared/tests/unit/conformance-vectors.test.js; the corpus vectors run); run via npm run test:shared.

Lock 3 (packages/shared/tests/unit/conformance-vectors.test.js) requires an eligible candidate recorded measured-unique (match_count 1, match_index 0) on every committed vector; run via npm run test:shared.

Every strategy definition on every platform must declare x-value-derived (packages/shared/tests/unit/schema-composition.test.js); scripts/sufficiency-lint.js enforces the masking the annotation promises.

The desktop role_name pinning in packages/shared/tests/unit/conformance-vectors.test.js exercises the non-localized binding over committed snapshots (localized name fields are placeholder-normalized, so a localized binding could not match); run via npm run test:shared.

packages/shared/tests/unit/no-resolution-execution.test.js guards the shipped surfaces, and the hygiene locks in packages/shared/tests/unit/conformance-vectors.test.js are structurally counts and committed-field equalities from which the "resolved" property emerges without being computed; run via npm run test:shared.

Intended: a consistency check asserting the authority-ordering statement stays consistent across its echo surfaces (this doc, docs/README.md, README.md, docs/requirements/replay-sufficiency.md, the sync reference README) — not built. Interim: review; the surfaces were hand-aligned in one change, and future edits to them should cite this clause.

Intended: a format prose-pin check comparing the three bump bullets to the classifier rule set (not yet built). Interim probe: the classifier side is pinned by its unit suite (packages/shared/tests/unit/classify-schema-change.test.js; run via npm run test:shared) — prose-to-code alignment stays a review judgment.

The allowlist and escalation live in scripts/classify-schema-change.js (PATCH_ON_ADD_ANNOTATIONS) with unit tests, and the x-value-derived drift guards pin the one known annotation against the real redaction code (packages/shared/tests/unit/schema-composition.test.js and the per-platform guards; run via npm run test:shared). The guard-coupling requirement for a future kind stays a review judgment — no check ties allowlist additions to guard existence.

scripts/check-no-release-outputs.js fails any feature-branch PR that edits schemas/dist/ or bumps a leaf delta version, so hand-bumping cannot merge; the release pipeline computes bumps via scripts/auto-version-schemas.js.

The exact five-field wrapper is asserted by packages/shared/tests/unit/contract.test.js (and the security suite); the three-field export is pinned by the desktop export suite validating buildExport output against the closed schema root (packages/desktop/tests/unit/export.test.js; npm run test:desktop) — the composed root is closed over exactly those three fields.

Intended: a pin requiring the shipped guidance asset to be re-confirmed whenever the schema source layers change (not built). Interim: review — whether a semantics change invalidates the guidance prose is judged per change.

Composition and validator tests derive stamps via stampFromSchema(composePlatform(...)) and pin the const behaviour; the backward-compat harness demonstrates the relaxation is harness-local (packages/shared/tests/unit/backward-compat.test.js; run via npm run test:shared).
The resolution procedure is implemented by resolveActiveSteps (packages/shared/lib/session.js) and pinned by the shared session unit tests; run via npm run test:shared.

The append-only mutations are pinned by the shared session unit tests, including the tombstone full-copy content and fresh created_at assertions added alongside this clause (packages/shared/tests/unit/session.test.js; run via npm run test:shared).
scripts/sufficiency-lint.js enforces this as the fail-class context-introduced predicate over every linted recording (npm run sufficiency:check).

clauses.justification: Whether a capture change preserves the describe-moment snapshot semantics is judgment; the conformance-vector hygiene locks probe recorded stats against committed snapshots (packages/shared/tests/unit/conformance-vectors.test.js).

clauses.check-ref: Intended: a pin asserting emitted locator order equals the schema strategy declaration order (not built). Interim probe: the scripted-truth corpus truths carry ordered locators, so an emitted-order change diffs against committed truth (npm run corpus:check and corpus:check:desktop).

Intended: a test pinning the unavailable-validator degradation (logged, unvalidated import) and the validate-before-persist ordering (not built). Existing coverage: the bounds, bounds-before-validation order, and validator delegation are pinned by packages/shared/tests/unit/validate-import.test.js; the stamp const gate by the composed-schema tests (packages/shared/tests/unit/schema-composition.test.js); both run via npm run test:shared.

Intended: a format prose-pin asserting the stated additionalProperties posture over the composed schemas (not yet built). Interim: the schemas are the authority; the composed-schema tests exercise the posture indirectly (npm run test:shared).

The background-layer lint rule (eslint.config.js, no-restricted-syntax ImportExpression) fails any dynamic import at lint time, and packages/extension/tests/unit/service-worker-static-import.test.js guards the entry module; run via npm run lint and npm run test:extension.

Intended: an end-to-end negative asserting no recorder is present on any page while no recording runs (not built). Interim probe: the injection e2e specs pin the positive half — injection on record-start and per-frame injection on load (packages/extension/tests/e2e/; npm run test:e2e).

The trust predicate is a pure module pinned by unit tests (packages/extension/lib/frame-trust.js, packages/extension/tests/unit/frame-trust.test.js; run via npm run test:extension), and the frame-trust e2e spec exercises the drop path.

Intended: an append-path unit assertion that a message-body context_id is ignored in favour of the sender-derived one (not built as a dedicated case). Interim probe: the service-worker unit suite exercises append stamping (npm run test:extension); the predicate module never reads a body context_id.

The injection-latency e2e spec measures from the navigation-complete signal (service-worker side) to the readiness timestamp the recorder stamps at ready, same machine wall clock, and enforces the half-floor bound (packages/extension/tests/e2e/specs/injection-latency.spec.js; npm run test:e2e).

Intended: a static check that recorder/service-worker event registrations stay within the enumerated surface (the CP-2 check family; not built). Interim probe: the scripted-truth corpus diff reddens when the surface drifts on scripted sessions (npm run corpus:check).

clauses.justification: Whether a proxy row still records the immediate effect of the chrome interaction it names is a per-proxy judgment (CP-7..CP-10 apply); the corpus ext-tab-open session probes the click-plus-chrome-navigate boundary.

clauses.check-ref: The corpus drives real trusted input only and its diff reddens if synthetic events enter a scripted session (scripts/corpus-compare.js via npm run corpus:check); the recorder unit tests pin the isTrusted rejection (npm run test:extension).

The corpus redaction session verifies mask + text-null + redacted end-to-end (npm run corpus:check); the shared detection util is unit-tested (npm run test:shared); scripts/sufficiency-lint.js enforces the locator masking half.

The url masked-parameter enumeration is pinned to the shared detection set by the schema composition test (packages/shared/tests/unit/schema-composition.test.js; npm run test:shared), and redactUrl is unit-tested (npm run test:shared).

The storage-quota unit tests pin the bands and hysteresis, and two e2e specs pin the pause/override/resume machine including live capture at the boundary (packages/extension/tests/e2e/specs/storage-quota.spec.js and storage-quota-live-capture.spec.js; npm run test:extension and test:e2e).

clauses.justification: Whether a proposed entry meets the admission test — the surface description alone would mislead without it — is judgment at review.

Whether a capture change preserves the thread-role split (hooks stay latency-bounded, describes stay on workers) is a design judgment; the input-thread accessibility scope is comment-pinned at its sites.

clauses.check-ref: Ordered insertion is pinned by the desktop reorder-buffer unit tests; the commit flush barrier — drain-and-continue on each worker, bounded fan-out with in-place rescue of a wedged worker, and the completion sentinel emitted last on the action stream — is pinned by the worker-pool flush tests (packages/desktop/src-tauri/tests/worker_pool_test.rs regression_298_*; npm run test:desktop:rust), and the frontend's wait for that sentinel by the commitWithCompleteness suite driving the real adapter against a mocked commit_barrier (npm run test:desktop).
The mode split and mixed recordings are pinned by the capture-mode Rust tests and the corpus coordinate session (npm run test:desktop:rust; npm run corpus:check:desktop).

Intended: a static check that hook/WinEvent registrations stay within the enumerated surface (the CP-2 check family; not built). Interim probe: the desktop corpus diff reddens when the surface drifts on scripted sessions (npm run corpus:check:desktop).

Intended: unit pins for the target-when-set filter and the exclusion-beats-target priority (not built). Existing coverage: the exclusion base rule and the destroyed-window skip are pinned by the pid-filter Rust tests, and the command normalization of 0/null by the commands tests (npm run test:desktop:rust).

clauses.justification: Whether a proxy row still records the immediate effect of the shell interaction it names is a per-proxy judgment (CP-7..CP-10 apply).

clauses.check-ref: The correlation gates are exercised end-to-end by the enigo integration suites (real input, real WinEvents) and probed by the desktop corpus sessions (npm run test:desktop:rust locally for the integration tier; npm run corpus:check:desktop).
The window-scoping rule is exercised by the capture integration tests (the Save As dialog pre-fill case) via npm run test:desktop:rust.
The buffering supersession is probed by the corpus type-edit session — its known-diff entries track the open supersession defects — via npm run corpus:check:desktop.

Intended: a dedicated classification unit over the press-move-release state machine (not built). Interim probe: the corpus click and double-click sessions pin the click paths (npm run corpus:check:desktop); the drag pair semantics are comment-pinned at the release handler.

The corpus redaction session verifies mask + text-null + redacted end-to-end on desktop (npm run corpus:check:desktop); the password path is pinned by the password Rust tests (npm run test:desktop:rust); scripts/sufficiency-lint.js enforces the locators-pass-untouched half.
The worker-pool invariants (sacred-buffer flush, panic respawn and retry, sticky value-change routing) are pinned by the worker-pool Rust tests (npm run test:desktop:rust).

clauses.justification: Whether a proposed entry meets the admission test — the surface description alone would mislead without it — is judgment at review.

clauses.check-ref: The reference server integration suite pins store-and-return-verbatim and no-server-side-state (reference-implementations/sync-server/tests/integration/protocol.test.js, reference-implementations/sync-server/tests/integration/round-trip.test.js; run by the server package test script in CI).

The whole-cycle 401/403 halt (before reconcile or push) is pinned by packages/shared/tests/unit/auth-failure-halt.property.test.js (npm run test:shared); the server-side token handling by reference-implementations/sync-server/tests/integration/auth.test.js.

Manifest shape and fields are pinned by reference-implementations/sync-server/tests/integration/protocol.test.js; the client-side connection test that reuses this endpoint by packages/shared/tests/unit/connection-test.test.js (npm run test:shared).

PUT semantics (path/body id match, verbatim replacement, 200/201) are pinned by reference-implementations/sync-server/tests/integration/protocol.test.js and reference-implementations/sync-server/tests/integration/round-trip.test.js.

Intended: a unit pinning that unrecognized top-level payload fields are ignored on pull (not built as a dedicated case). Interim: the pull path reconstructs projects through an allowlist, so unknown fields are dropped by construction (packages/shared/sync-client.js).
The pull-before-push ordering and its divergence-detection consequence are pinned by packages/shared/tests/unit/pull-precedes-push.property.test.js (npm run test:shared).

The capture halt by packages/shared/tests/unit/capture-active-halt.property.test.js, the pending-actions halt by packages/shared/tests/unit/pending-action-protection.property.test.js, and the inbound locked exclusion by packages/shared/tests/unit/locked-recording-exclusion.property.test.js (npm run test:shared).

The stamp gate (skip-and-report, never a conflict, per-project) is pinned by packages/shared/tests/unit/stamp-compatibility.test.js; pulled-payload schema validation (invalid is an error, never a conflict, per-project) by packages/shared/tests/unit/safeguard-composition.property.test.js (npm run test:shared).

The digest-based classification is pinned by packages/shared/tests/unit/classify-decision-table.property.test.js and packages/shared/tests/unit/classify-deterministic.property.test.js (npm run test:shared).
Baseline advancement (agreement/adoption only, never push) is pinned by packages/shared/tests/unit/baseline-advancement.property.test.js (npm run test:shared).

The two conflict-producing situations (and only those) are pinned by packages/shared/tests/unit/divergence-conflict.property.test.js, packages/shared/tests/unit/delete-vs-change-conflict.property.test.js, and packages/shared/tests/unit/no-baseline-no-one-sided-change.property.test.js (npm run test:shared).

The identity-based append-only-and-nothing-else predicate is pinned by packages/shared/tests/unit/fast-forward-superset.property.test.js; the deletions toggle’s auto-apply outcomes by packages/shared/tests/unit/reported-counts.property.test.js (npm run test:shared).

Per-recording assembly, the no-omission rule, deferred/locked version selection, and the content-decided skip are pinned by packages/shared/tests/unit/per-unit-push-assembly.property.test.js, packages/shared/tests/unit/locked-recording-push-preservation.property.test.js, and packages/shared/tests/unit/changed-local-outgoing-push.property.test.js (npm run test:shared).

The stored-project If-Match rows (match proceeds with fresh ETag; stale 412 leaves the store unchanged) are pinned by reference-implementations/sync-server/tests/integration/conditional-write.test.js; the no-project-stored 412 branch is pinned at the decision-function level by reference-implementations/sync-server/tests/unit/conditional-write.test.js (an end-to-end case for that branch is a named gap).

clauses.justification: Whether a truth edit derives from the script and the capture principles rather than enshrining recorder output is judgment at the mandatory line-by-line review.

Whether a missing fact is format-expressible (corpus territory) or not (the sufficiency lint’s gap territory) is a per-fact judgment; truth files’ schema validity is enforced by their membership in the lint corpus (npm run sufficiency:check).

clauses.check-ref: The both-direction baseline lock is implemented by scripts/corpus-compare.js and pinned by packages/shared/tests/unit/corpus-compare.test.js (npm run test:shared); it gates CI via npm run corpus:check and corpus:check:desktop.

Intended: a page lint rejecting external resources and time/locale-dependent constructs in committed session pages (not built). Interim: corpus/serve.js pins the fixed loopback port, and pages are reviewed against the authoring rules.

The closed kinds, strategy cross-check, masked-entry refusal, the 0-exact scroll class map, and the every-entry-applies validation are implemented by scripts/corpus-compare.js and pinned by packages/shared/tests/unit/corpus-compare.test.js (npm run test:shared); the alignment-scoping behaviour and the CLI exit-code mapping stay review-held.

clauses.justification: Whether a new page obeys the determinism rules is review judgment; cross-run stability of the corpus in CI is the operational probe.
Whether a driver API produces trusted input on a novel interaction is judgment; a violation surfaces as a missing action in the session’s own diff.

clauses.check-ref: The pipeline runs end-to-end on producer-generated dumps: the Rust corpus test produces them (locally on Windows; the Windows CI job uploads them as an artifact), then npm run corpus:assemble:desktop replays them through the real adapter and npm run corpus:check:desktop diffs against committed truth.

clauses.justification: Whether a new session’s design stays environment-independent (no programmatic raise, click-driven activation) is judgment; CI retry-budget stability is the operational probe.

clauses.check-ref: The produce-stage oracle asserts produced == committed at the two production sites (packages/extension/tests/e2e/corpus/corpus.spec.js exact; scripts/corpus-assemble-desktop-vectors.js under the comparator class rules), and the hygiene locks re-derive the committed vectors’ counts (packages/shared/tests/unit/conformance-vectors.test.js; npm run test:shared).

The locks are counts and equalities by construction, and packages/shared/tests/unit/no-resolution-execution.test.js guards that no shipped surface implements the ordered procedure (npm run test:shared).

The ledger lock ties every emitted strategy to a committed vector or a reasoned gap (corpus/vectors-coverage.json, locked in packages/shared/tests/unit/conformance-vectors.test.js; npm run test:shared).

clauses.justification: The coverage boundary is a scope statement; whether a change moves behaviour across it (into the unexercised bridge, UI, or persistence surfaces) is judgment.
Whether a retirement reason is honest and a removal decision is the maintainer’s are process judgments no check can decide.

clauses.check-ref: The desktop hygiene locks run over the committed placeholder-normalized snapshots, so a snapshot freezing an OS-locale Name diverges from the locks’ re-derivations (packages/shared/tests/unit/conformance-vectors.test.js; npm run test:shared); the normalization itself is applied by scripts/corpus-assemble-desktop-vectors.js.

clauses.justification: Whether a vector’s element facts were captured through the real desktop path rather than hand-authored is provenance judgment at review; lock 2 checks internal consistency, not origin.

clauses.check-ref: Lock 2 permits exactly the labeled_by/tree_path additive-stats augmentation and nothing else, and lock 5 re-derives the harness-measured pairs over the committed snapshot (packages/shared/tests/unit/conformance-vectors.test.js; npm run test:shared).

clauses.justification: Whether a produced-vs-committed divergence is an environment variance the comparator class rules absorb or a deliberate re-baseline moment (a runner-image bump) is judgment; the produce-stage oracle reddens either way.
